name: Arterial telomere uncapping and p53/p21 senescence were higher in hypertensive adults
summary: Human disease-context data link arterial telomere uncapping to hypertension-associated senescence signals.
statement: In age-matched adults, hypertensive participants had greater arterial telomere uncapping and p53/p21 senescence signaling than nonhypertensive participants despite no significant arterial mean telomere-length difference.
population_or_model: Human artery samples from hypertensive and nonhypertensive adults
quantitative_note: The source reports approximately twofold greater arterial telomere uncapping and P53/P21-induced senescence in hypertensive participants.
caveats: The finding is associative and disease-contextual.
It does not show that telomere-capping intervention prevents, reverses, or treats hypertension.
